sketches.mechanism: The Maybe monad's >>= operator silently propagates Nothing through an entire chain, so once any step yields Nothing, every subsequent step is skipped without error.
sketches.resonance: Once someone disappears from a sequence of shared moments, every future moment quietly becomes Nothing too.
sketches.draft:
together :: Maybe a
together = return () >>= \_ ->
  Just () >>= \_ ->
  Just () >>= \_ ->
  Nothing >>= \_ ->
  Just () >>= \_ ->
  Just ()

sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates exactly one instance that can never be duplicated or reconstructed — a language-enforced singleton.
sketches.resonance: Some moments exist only once; the language itself forbids their repetition.

sketches.mechanism: A `volatile` variable tells the compiler the value may change at any moment without warning, so it must be re-read every single time — it can never be cached or assumed stable.
sketches.resonance: Some presences must be checked again and again because you can never trust they'll still be there.
